commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=20b124ce4e189f52ab650127dc360ad6313be3aa)
commands.review_summary:
The PR replaces iMessage RPC stdout `node:readline` parsing with an LF-only `StringDecoder` buffer and adds client tests for Unicode separator and split UTF-8 responses.

PR surface: Source +8, Tests +339. Total +347 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source/dependency level: current main and `v2026.6.1` use readline on `imsg rpc` stdout, and a local Node probe shows U+2028 splits into invalid JSON fragments. The new shutdown regression is also source-reproducible with a late stdout event after `decoder` is cleared.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainer or contributor follow-up is needed because the PR has a concrete shutdown bug and the external real-environment proof gate cannot be satisfied by an automated repair worker alone.

target.title: fix(imessage): replace node:readline with LF-only buffer to prevent U+2028 splitting JSON-RPC responses
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #89830  Node readline treats U+2028/U+2029 as line terminators, splitting a single JSON-RPC response into fragments that all fail JSON.parse and block all iMessage receives (head-of-line blocking). Additionally, per-chunk `Buffer.toString("utf-8")` silently corrupts multi-byte characters (emoji, CJK) split across stdout chunk boundaries into replacement characters ().

Replace node:readline with manual \n-only stdout buffering backed by `StringDecoder` for cross-chunk UTF-8 reassembly. U+2028/U+2029 inside JSON string values survive intact; split multi-byte sequences are reassembled correctly.

## Tests

16 tests in extensions/imessage/src/client.test.ts:
- U+2028/U+2029 preserved inside JSON strings (not split as line terminators)
- LF framing: multi-response split, cross-chunk buffering, empty line skip
- handleLine: resolve, reject, notification dispatch, parse error logging
- Cross-chunk UTF-8 (#89883): emoji, CJK, U+2028 split at byte boundaries reassembled by StringDecoder; naive toString produces replacement characters
- Realistic messages.history response with U+2028 resolves pending request

## Validation

pnpm test extensions/imessage/src/client.test.ts → 16 passed
pnpm test extensions/imessage/src/status.test.ts → 12 passed

## Real behavior proof

- macOS 15.7.7, M4 MacBook Air, Node v22.22.3, imsg v0.11.0
- Gateway starts and receives/sends messages via iMessage normally
- macOS Messages.app normalizes U+2028 at the UI layer before it reaches chat.db, so real U+2028 delivery cannot be verified end-to-end; unit tests directly cover the framing code path
- Not tested: real iMessage containing raw U+2028 (filtered by Apple client)
